meaning: n.快步舞,快步舞曲,带锤子的钓钩,夹具v.乱跳,加工用钩钓鱼,选筛
examples: The cooking process is so theatrical and you'll feel like dancing a jig while you're eating it.
As a last thought, he pulled out his fiddle as he ran, and struck up a jig, turning to the bull, and backing towards the corner.
The likelihood of finding a Salomon mounting jig in Chogoria Hospital seems rather remote.
Steel is stretched and shrunk using planishing hammers, jig mills and Yoder roll-forming machines.
